case_title: ROBERT LALANNE VS. MICHELE ARMANINO et al

docket_entries.description: PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY PETITIONER LALANNE, ROBERT AS TO RESPONDENT ARMANINO, MICHELE LALANNE LLC JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER ROBERT LALANNE'S NOTICE OF PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS CONFIRMED. PURSUANT TO CCP 1286, THE COURT WILL CONFIRM THE AWARD "AS MADE." THE COURT DENIES PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO ENTER TWO JUDGMENTS THAT ALTER THE AWARD; NOR WILL THE COURT ADDRESS OTHER ISSUES CONCERNING THE ENFORCEMENT OR INTERPRETATION OF THE AWARD. THE COURT WILL SIGN A FORM OF JUDGMENT SIMPLY CONFIRMING THE AWARD AND WITH THE AWARD ATTACHED AS ITS EXHIBIT A. THE COURT DECLINES TO STAY EXECUTION OF THE JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 918(A). THE COURT NOTES WITH DISPLEASURE THAT PETITIONER FILED A 26-PAGE REPLY BRIEF WITHOUT PERMISSION IN FLAGRANT DISREGARD OF CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1113(D), (E), AND (F). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Petitioner Robert Lalanne'S Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRIS ANDREAS, ESQ., 415.828.5624, FOR PETITIONER ROBERT LALANNE.
ROGER MEAD, ESQ., 415.625.1050, FOR RESPONDENT MICHELE ARMANIN0.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Petitioner Robert Lalanne's Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is confirmed. Pursuant to CCP 1286, the Court will confirm the award "as made." The Court denies Petitioner's request to enter two judgments that alter the award; nor will the Court address other issues concerning the enforcement or interpretation of the award. The Court will sign a form of judgment simply confirming the award and with the award attached as its Exhibit A. The Court declines to stay execution of the judgment pursuant to CCP 918(a). The Court notes with displeasure that Petitioner filed a 26-page reply brief without permission in flagrant disregard of California Rules of Court 3.1113(d), (e), and (f). Order signed in open court. Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
